rationale: Title Y is substantially more informative: it names the comparison group (with vs. without prior sternotomy), states the key finding ('comparable'), specifies the primary endpoints (sensing, defibrillation thresholds, complications), and provides the sample size (212 patients). PACE readers and reviewers immediately grasp the study design, scope, and conclusion. Title X describes only a broad topic ('safety and outcomes') without revealing the comparator arm or any finding, making it less useful as a standalone descriptor.
loser_weakness: Title X omits the comparison group (with vs. without prior sternotomy), leaves findings unstated, and uses vague endpoint language ('safety and outcomes'), reducing both informativeness and appeal for a device-oriented PACE audience.
